channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump arrives in France for G7 leaders summit, touting peace deal with Iran
item.description: French president Emmanuel Macron met with Trump and congratulated him for signing the new Iran agreement.
item.content:encoded: French president Emmanuel Macron met with Trump and congratulated him for signing the new Iran agreement.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: 'The Tuxedo Society,' a fun thriller that imagines an LGBTQ spy agency
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Paul Rudnick about his new novel, The Tuxedo Society.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Paul Rudnick about his new novel, The Tuxedo Society.
item.dc:creator: Elena Burnett

item.title: The Knicks run sparked a 'collective effervescence' in New York, grief specialist says
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with New Yorker and grief specialist Barri Leiner Grant, who has written about how the New York Knicks championship run sparked a "collective effervescence" in the city.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with New Yorker and grief specialist Barri Leiner Grant, who has written about how the New York Knicks championship run sparked a "collective effervescence" in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Why a brain research lab is switching from fruit flies to tiny fish
item.description: One of the world's leading brain research labs is switching from fruit flies to a tiny, transparent fish. The goal is to observe an entire animal's brain at work.
item.content:encoded: One of the world's leading brain research labs is switching from fruit flies to a tiny, transparent fish. The goal is to observe an entire animal's brain at work.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: U.K. announces social media bad for children under 16
item.description: The UK government has announced it will ban social media for all children under 16. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was "giving children their childhood back."
item.content:encoded: The UK government has announced it will ban social media for all children under 16. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was "giving children their childhood back."
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: Iran's soccer team was welcomed in Tijuana after being blocked from training in U.S.
item.description: While the U.S. has rejected the Iran soccer team, forcing them to train in Tijuana during this world cup, the border city has rolled out a welcome for a squad caught between sport and geopolitics.
item.content:encoded: While the U.S. has rejected the Iran soccer team, forcing them to train in Tijuana during this world cup, the border city has rolled out a welcome for a squad caught between sport and geopolitics.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: How much of an economic boom is the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the U.S. hosting cities?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is thought to bring an economic windfall to the cities and regions where games will be played during four weeks this summer. How true is that?
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup is thought to bring an economic windfall to the cities and regions where games will be played during four weeks this summer. How true is that?
item.dc:creator: Dylan Duke

item.title: Inside Ebola country: NPR reports from eastern DR Congo's outbreak zone
item.description: In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, an Ebola outbreak is reshaping life, with fear spreading faster than information and hospitals turning into places of urgent containment rather than care.
item.content:encoded: In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, an Ebola outbreak is reshaping life, with fear spreading faster than information and hospitals turning into places of urgent containment rather than care.
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Newsom points fingers at DOJ
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom claims the Trump administration is targeting him and his wife.
item.content:encoded: California Gov. Gavin Newsom claims the Trump administration is targeting him and his wife.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: 'Sometimes splitting is a good thing': An Illinois movement aims to be the 51st state
item.description: A third of Illinois counties have passed resolutions seeking to separate from the city of Chicago. People in these rural counties resent what they see as disproportionate power wielded by Chicago.
item.content:encoded: A third of Illinois counties have passed resolutions seeking to separate from the city of Chicago. People in these rural counties resent what they see as disproportionate power wielded by Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Connor O'Neill
